description: RxHARM Indicator Registry - metadata for all HVI sub-index indicators

lst.name: Landsat Land Surface Temperature
lst.citation: USGS Landsat Collection 2, ESPA Surface Reflectance
lst.notes: 90th percentile composite over hottest N_HOTTEST_MONTHS period. Cloud masked using QA_PIXEL bits 3 and 4.

albedo.name: Landsat Broadband Albedo
albedo.citation: Liang (2000), Remote Sensing of Environment, Vol 76(1):213-238
albedo.notes: Direction=negative means low albedo = high vulnerability (more heat absorption). Applied to scaled surface reflectance (multiply by 0.0000275, add -0.2).

uhi.name: Urban Heat Island Intensity
uhi.citation: Zhou et al. (2019), Science Advances; derived from Landsat LST composite
uhi.notes: Rural background computed from non-built pixels (Dynamic World label not in [6]) within a 5km moving window. Requires Dynamic World for urban mask.

population.name: WorldPop Population Count
population.citation: WorldPop Global2 (2025), worldpop.org; Bondarenko et al. (2025)
population.notes: Use GEE native collection for years 2000-2020. Use community catalog Global2 for 2021-2030. Annual estimates based on dasymetric redistribution of census data.

built_frac.name: GHS Built-Up Surface Fraction
built_frac.citation: JRC GHS-BUILT-S R2023A, Pesaresi et al. (2023)
built_frac.notes: Used both as exposure modifier (built cells carry more exposure weight) and as feasibility constraint for cool roof and pavement interventions.

elderly_frac.name: Elderly Population Fraction (65+)
elderly_frac.citation: WorldPop constrained age-sex (2020 GEE native); Global2 annual for other years
elderly_frac.notes: Elderly populations have reduced thermoregulatory capacity. Source: Bunker et al. (2016), EBioMedicine.

child_frac.name: Child Population Fraction (<5 years)
child_frac.notes: Children under 5 have immature thermoregulation and depend on caregivers for adaptive behavior.

impervious.name: Impervious Surface Fraction (Dynamic World Built)
impervious.citation: Brown et al. (2022), Scientific Data; Google/WRI Dynamic World V1
impervious.notes: High impervious fraction reduces evaporative cooling from the urban environment, increasing heat stress for all occupants.

cropland.name: Cropland Fraction (Agricultural Worker Proxy)
cropland.notes: Proxy for mandatory outdoor exposure. Agricultural workers face heat stress during productive hours with no indoor refuge.

ndvi.name: Sentinel-2 NDVI (Vegetation Greenness)
ndvi.citation: ESA Copernicus Sentinel-2; Tucker (1979), Remote Sensing of Environment
ndvi.notes: Higher NDVI = more photosynthetically active vegetation = higher evapotranspiration cooling. AC indicator so direction=positive means high NDVI = high AC = lower vulnerability.

tree_cover.name: Hansen GFW Effective Tree Cover
tree_cover.citation: Hansen et al. (2013), Science; Global Forest Watch
tree_cover.notes: Effective tree cover accounts for forest loss since 2000. Loss years encoded as years since 2000 (1=2001). Captures persistent woody canopy, not seasonal vegetation.

canopy_height.name: Potapov/GLAD Global Forest Canopy Height
canopy_height.citation: Potapov et al. (2021), Remote Sensing of Environment, Vol 253:112165
canopy_height.notes: Shade quality indicator: tall canopy provides fundamentally better thermal relief than low vegetation with equivalent coverage. Known limitation: saturates >30m, may confuse buildings with trees in urban cores.

ndwi.name: Sentinel-2 NDWI (Vegetation Moisture)
ndwi.citation: Gao (1996), Remote Sensing of Environment; McFeeters vegetation moisture variant
ndwi.notes: IMPORTANT: This is the vegetation moisture NDWI (NIR-SWIR1)/(NIR+SWIR1), NOT the open water NDWI (Green-NIR)/(Green+NIR). Captures plant water stress during pre-monsoon dry period when NDVI may still show green but ET cooling is suppressed.

viirs_dnb.name: VIIRS DNB Nighttime Light (Downscaled to 100m)
viirs_dnb.downscaling_citation: Jeswani et al. (2023), International Journal of Applied Earth Observation and Geoinformation, 122:103395
viirs_dnb.citation: Elvidge et al. (2017), International Journal of Remote Sensing
viirs_dnb.notes: Downscaled from 500m to 100m using Random Forest area-to-point regression Kriging with WorldPop as the fine-resolution covariate. Economic and electrification proxy for adaptive capacity. Known limitations: saturation in dense commercial cores, light blooming from adjacent bright sources.
